---
title: ""
page-layout: full
---

::: {.container}
::: {.about-grid}

::: {}
### Max Avramis

**javramis@ucsd.edu**  
Chicago, IL (relocating June 2026)  

[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/maxavramis) · [GitHub](https://github.com/javramis)

---

**Education**

UC San Diego, GPS  
*Master of Public Policy* · 2026  
Business Regulation & Government Innovation

Pace University  
*Bachelor of Fine Arts* · 2020

---

**Skills**

::: {.skills-list}
<span>R</span><span>Python</span><span>QGIS</span><span>Google Earth Engine</span><span>SQL</span><span>GitHub</span><span>Quarto</span><span>ggplot2</span><span>caret</span><span>metafor</span><span>tidyverse</span><span>XGBoost</span><span>Random Forest</span><span>Excel</span><span>SharePoint</span>
:::

**Languages**

::: {.skills-list}
<span>English (native)</span><span>Spanish (intermediate)</span><span>Greek (beginner)</span>
:::
:::

::: {}
## About

I came to policy school from a career as a professional actor, which left me with a particular sensitivity to audience — to whether an idea is actually being communicated, not just being said.

What really drew me into GIS and data science was realizing these tools could do something no other analytical framework could: make a rigorous, multi-variable argument legible to essentially anyone in any room. I had spent a year building a foundation in quantitative policy analysis — cost-effectiveness, benefit-cost analysis, and my personal favorite, multi-criteria decision analysis. GIS gave me the communication layer. Combining them felt like gaining a universal language.

That instinct runs through everything I build: the question is never just *"is this accurate?"* but *"will this land for the person making the decision?"*

---

## Research Focus

My work sits at the intersection of **spatial analysis**, **machine learning**, and **policy research** — with a particular interest in:

- Climate and disaster risk mapping (flood hazards, wildfire vulnerability)
- Global health infrastructure and pandemic preparedness
- Education technology and learning inequality
- Labor markets and AI regulation

---

## Experience

**Health Forecasting Alliance (VitalWave)** · Strategy & Research Consultant · *Jan 2026–Present*  
UCSD GPS Capstone with Brooke Partridge, CEO of VitalWave

**The Basement, UCSD (Blackstone LaunchPad)** · i4X Innovation Fellow · *Dec 2025–Present*  
Capital Program Management AI tool for UCSD Healthcare

**The Asia Foundation** · Programs Intern · *Jul–Aug 2025*  
Innovation & Impact Fund dashboard; Let's Read donor strategy

**UC San Diego, Dept. of Theatre & Dance** · Teaching Assistant · *FA 2025–SP 2026*  
Theatre 101; 4.75/5 teaching evaluation

**US Embassy Luxembourg** · Personal Assistant to the Ambassador · *2018, 2020*  
Recipient of the Ambassador's Award

---

## Download CV

[Download CV (PDF)]{.cta-primary style="padding: 0.6rem 1.4rem; background: #1a1a2e; color: #fff; border-radius: 2px; font-size: 0.85rem; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; text-decoration: none; display: inline-block; margin-top: 1rem;"}

:::
:::
:::
